formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Since the chapter 'Constitution as a Living Document' is a part of Political Science, it is more focused on concepts and principles rather than mathematical formulas. Here's a JSON array with key statements and laws related to the chapter:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Separation of Powers",
    "formula": "Separation of Powers: Legislative, Executive, and Judicial",
    "variables": "Legislative (Parliament/Legislature), Executive (Council of Ministers), and Judicial (Supreme Court)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The Constitution establishes a system of checks and balances to prevent any one branch of government from becoming too powerful."
  },
  {
    "name": "Rule of Law",
    "formula": "The Constitution is the supreme law of the land.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The Constitution is the foundation of the rule of law, which ensures that all citizens are equal before the law and are protected by it."
  },
  {
    "name": "Fundamental Rights",
    "formula": "The Constitution guarantees six fundamental rights: Right to Equality, Right to Freedom, Right against Exploitation, Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression, Right to Education, and Right to Constitutional Remedies.",
    "variables": "Article 14 (Right to Equality), Article 15 (Right to Freedom), Article 17 (Right against Exploitation), Article 19 (Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression), Article 21 (Right to Education), Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "These rights are fundamental to the well-being and dignity of citizens and are essential for a democratic society."
  },
  {
    "name": "Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP)",
    "formula": "DPSP: Socialism, Secularism, and Welfare State",
    "variables": "Article 39 (Socialism), Article 51-A (Secularism), Article 38 (Welfare State)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The DPSP are guidelines for the state to follow in order to promote social welfare and economic prosperity."
  },
  {
    "name": "Amendment Procedure",
    "formula": "Amendment Procedure: Parliament can amend the Constitution through a two-thirds majority in both Houses or through a special amendment procedure.",
    "variables": "Article 368 (Amendment Procedure)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The amendment procedure ensures that any changes to the Constitution are made in a fair and democratic manner."
  },
  {
    "name": "Preamble",
    "formula": "The Preamble sets out the objectives and ideals of the Constitution.",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The Preamble is a guiding light for the interpretation of the Constitution and serves as a reminder of the country's values and principles."
  }
]
```

Note: Since this chapter is more focused on concepts and principles rather than mathematical formulas, the above JSON array contains key statements and laws related to the chapter.
